label: 3444 Transfer of programs from National Science Foundation

children.label: B
children.text: the continuing education of scientific personnel;

children.label: C
children.text: increasing the participation of women, minorities, and the handicapped in careers in science;

children.label: D
children.text: the conduct of basic and applied research and development applied to science learning at all educational levels and the dissemination of results concerning such research and development; and

children.label: E
children.text: informing the general public of the nature of science and technology and of attendant values and public policy issues.
Except as provided in paragraph (1), no mission oriented research functions or programs of the National Science Foundation or any other Federal agency shall be transferred by this chapter.

children.heading: Authority of Secretary; consultation and advice
children.label: b Authority of Secretary; consultation and advice
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to conduct the programs transferred by subsection (a). In conducting such programs the Secretary shall consult, as appropriate, with the Director of the National Science Foundation, and shall establish advisory mechanisms designed to assure that scientists and engineers are fully involved in the development, implementation, and review of science education programs.

children.heading: Report by Secretary
children.label: c Report by Secretary
children.text: The annual report to be transmitted by the Secretary pursuant to shall include a description of arrangements, developed by the Secretary in consultation with the Director of the National Science Foundation, for coordinated planning and operation of science education programs, including measures to facilitate the implementations of successful innovations.section 3486 of this title

children.heading: Reservation of certain Foundation authority
children.label: d Reservation of certain Foundation authority
children.text: Nothing in this section is intended to repeal or limit the authority of the National Science Foundation or the Director of the National Science Foundation to initiate and conduct programs under the National Science Foundation Act of 1950 [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 1861
